title: Six primitives as atomic foundation
decision: Everything in the OS is built from six atomic primitives: Handshake, Attestation, Function, Organisation, Forward Contract, Payment Waterfall. If a new feature cannot be expressed as a composition of these primitives, either the feature is wrong or a primitive is missing.
rationale: Composability over complexity. Proven patterns from CCSMP (handshake) extended to full value chain.

title: Capability-based access control over role-based
decision: Access control uses capabilities (what you can do) + scope (how far it reaches) instead of fixed roles. Presets are convenience labels only. One RLS policy per table checks auth.has_capability().
rationale: Adding a new participant type requires zero code changes — just capability assignment. Roles are rigid; capabilities compose.

title: Financial privacy — nobody sees the farmer's money
decision: Extension officers and implementing partners are NEVER granted financial.view. They see farming performance, not money. District+ scope sees aggregates only. This prevents rent-seeking.
rationale: The closer you are to the farmer, the more you see about their farming — but nobody at any level sees their money.

title: One member, one vote — always
decision: Trust score gates role eligibility (e.g. cluster leader requires Established tier), not voting weight. Every cluster member gets exactly one vote regardless of trust score, volume, or seniority.
rationale: Democratic legitimacy of cluster governance. Trust measures reliability, not authority.

title: Right to exit — unconditional
decision: Any cluster member can exit at any time. Money follows the member (pending distributions paid out, treasury share calculated). Outstanding obligations (input advances) also follow.
rationale: Voluntary participation is non-negotiable. Captive cooperatives fail. Exit rights create accountability pressure on leadership.

title: Handshake is sacred — do not add steps
decision: The handshake primitive is exactly two parties independently verifying the same event. Do not add approvers, workflows, or additional steps. If they agree, confirmed. If they disagree, both claims preserved and transaction blocks.
rationale: Proven in CCSMP. Complexity kills adoption. The handshake is the atomic unit of trust.

title: Attestations unify all real-world claims
decision: Training sessions, monitoring visits, advisory interactions, quality tests, and partner service delivery are ALL stored via the attestation primitive. No separate tables. attestation_type distinguishes them.
rationale: Every claim about a real-world event goes through the same structure with the same optional corroboration and the same Merkle tree inclusion.

title: Payment waterfall priority order — fixed, versioned, multi-sig to change
decision: Priority: 1. Warehouse custody fee, 2. SAFF loan repayment (pro-rata), 3. Input supplier repayment (pro-rata), 4. Insurance premium, 5. Platform fee, 6. Farmer net → cluster treasury → mobile money.
rationale: Deterministic, zero human discretion in routing. Farmer gets the residual. Secured creditors (warehouse, bank, supplier) paid first to make the system bankable.

title: Trust score visibility — tier only, never the number
decision: Counterparties see trust tier (Verified/Established/Trusted/Anchor) but never the numeric score (0-100). Only the org itself sees their own number + breakdown.
rationale: Prevents gaming. Tiers provide enough signal for contract decisions without creating a score-optimization culture.

title: Four-tier conflict resolution with SLAs
decision: Tier 1: automatic (24hr SLA, ≤2% handshake discrepancy). Tier 2: mediation by extension officer (14 days). Tier 3: 3-person arbitration panel, binding, creates precedent (30 days). Tier 4: account suspension, evidence package, referral to ACC/ZPS/BoZ/courts.
rationale: Escalation by severity. Most disputes resolve automatically or through mediation. Arbitration creates case law. External enforcement is last resort.

title: Multi-sig treasury thresholds
decision: Below K2,000: leader alone. K2,000-K10,000: leader + treasurer. Above K10,000: two of three (leader, treasurer, depot witness). Thresholds configurable.
rationale: Proportional control. Small operational expenses don't need committee approval. Large withdrawals need independent witness.

title: Ejection requires arbitration ruling or court order
decision: Permanent removal from the system (ejection) cannot be done by platform administrators. It requires either a Tier 3 arbitration ruling or a court order. Not reversible by admin.
rationale: No single point of power can remove a participant. Due process is non-negotiable.

title: Tech stack: React + Supabase + Netlify
decision: Frontend: React + Tailwind CSS (PWA). Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime + Edge Functions + Storage). Hosting: Netlify. Mobile money: Airtel/MTN APIs. SMS/USSD: Africa's Talking or Zamtel.
rationale: Same stack as proven CCSMP. RLS for access control. Real-time subscriptions. Edge Functions for async processing.

title: Three auth methods by participant type
decision: Google OAuth for institutional users (government, processors, insurers, banks). SIM OTP as baseline for field users. Mobile money binding preferred for farmers — identity + payment destination + inherited telco KYC.
rationale: Meet users where they are. Farmers have mobile money. Government staff have Google accounts. Nobody needs to create a new identity.

title: Audit log is append-only, no UPDATE or DELETE — ever
decision: The audit_log table has no UPDATE or DELETE policies. Every action is recorded permanently. No exceptions, no admin override.
rationale: The audit trail is the foundation of trust. If it can be edited, nothing built on it can be trusted.

title: General-purpose modular platform — CATSP is configuration, not code
decision: Platform modules (Contracts, Verification, Treasury, Governance, Reputation, Disputes, Integrity, Field Ops) are general-purpose tools. CATSP-specific logic (commodities, grading standards, waterfall priority, preset definitions, governance thresholds) lives in database configuration rows, not in application code. The same engine can run agricultural value chains, NGO impact measurement, project management, or cooperative governance by changing configuration data.
rationale: Modularity and reusability. Each module ships independently. Adding a new use case requires configuration, not code changes. Like Odoo — the modules are industry-agnostic, the configuration is industry-specific.

title: Monorepo with pnpm workspaces — apps, packages, supabase
decision: Single repository with pnpm workspaces. Three top-level directories: apps/ (web PWA, USSD gateway, presentation site), packages/ (shared types, offline sync, USSD flows), supabase/ (migrations by module, Edge Functions, seed data). TypeScript throughout.
rationale: Simplest monorepo tool. Shared code (capabilities, waterfall math, validation) used by all channels (PWA, USSD, Edge Functions) without duplication. Module migrations numbered by domain (00_foundation, 10_contracts, 20_verification, etc.) for clear dependency ordering.

title: Offline-first with action queue and server reconciliation
decision: PWA uses IndexedDB (Dexie.js) for offline data cache and action queue. Write actions (handshake confirmations, votes) queued locally and synced when connected. Read data is last-write-wins from server. Conflict resolution: expired votes rejected with SMS notification, duplicate handshakes flagged, stale balances overwritten. Background Sync API triggers reconciliation.
rationale: Rural Zambia has 2G connectivity, frequent drops, solar-charged phones. Online-first with offline fallback won't work. Must be offline-first with sync-when-connected.
